common_constraints.comparison_scope: Do not mix protocol tiers in a quantitative barrier comparison; all tiers require an independent C13-C21 mode review.
common_constraints.standard_state: Gaussian default ideal-gas 1 atm for the isolated TS/Freq evidence

comparison_notes: The standard option is the primary one-job reproduction candidate; loose weakens numerical controls, while strict adds a separately reviewed Hessian diagnostic.
All three retain the same literature method and solvent so the choice isolates numerical protocol rather than changing the electronic-structure model.
Difficulty, protocol rigor, and the complex 120 GB/44-core resource proposal remain separate decisions.

drivers: low-magnitude literature imaginary frequency near -352 cm-1
carbon-carbon bond-forming coordinate with a 2.22 angstrom starting distance
evidence: Hash-bound SI geometry and expected result in candidate-ledger.json
BF3-TS1 completed at the same user-selected base method and solvent
BF3-TS2-B2 remains a fresh candidate requiring its own protocol selection and exact approval
BF3-TS2-B1 has completed numerically but its C13-C21 mode decision remains pending

non_claims: Strict is not an accuracy guarantee and does not prove the chemical model.
No option authorizes input rendering, server-directory creation, submission, retry, IRC, cancellation, or cleanup.
This proposal does not treat the numerical B1 result as an accepted mode decision and cannot bypass that prerequisite.
Agreement with the reported imaginary frequency would not by itself validate the normal mode or reaction path.

prerequisites: explicit approval of the neutral-singlet hypothesis
reviewed 78-atom literature geometry and C13-C21 atom map
accepted hash-bound BF3-TS2-B1 C13-C21 mode decision before rendering a B2 input
applicability.reason: Useful as a fast geometry-recovery attempt when the published structure is already close to the intended saddle.

limitations: Default geometry convergence and grid provide weaker numerical evidence than the standard option.
No IRC or endpoint identity is included.

frequency_controls.type: analytic harmonic frequencies at the final geometry

method_profiles.relativistic_treatment: not required for B, C, F, H, N, and O

scf.convergence: default convergence with XQC fallback proposed
scf.stability_check: not included in the primary optimization

method_profiles.software_compatibility: reviewed for Gaussian 16 A.03 main-group closed-shell execution

provenance: User-selected omega-B97XD/6-31G(d), SMD toluene base method for the BF3 benchmark sequence.
Published BF3-TS2-B2 Cartesian geometry and C13-C21 target from DOI 10.1021/jacs.4c09067 SI.
options.purpose: Lowest-cost direct single-guess TS reoptimization from the published Cartesian geometry, followed by an analytic harmonic frequency calculation.

assumptions: Qualitative estimate only; convergence behavior dominates wall time.
Protocol rigor and resource tier remain independent decisions.

acceptance_checks: fresh hash-bound manual review against C13-C21 bond formation

validation_plan.claim_limit: Diagnostic mode-consistent TS only; default numerical convergence is not the primary reproduction protocol.

exclusions: IRC without a separate direction-specific approval
prerequisites: explicit approval of the neutral-singlet hypothesis
reviewed 78-atom literature geometry and C13-C21 atom map
accepted hash-bound BF3-TS2-B1 C13-C21 mode decision before rendering a B2 input
applicability.reason: Balances numerical control and cost for the second BF3 carbon-carbon bond-forming topology.

coverage_plan.sensitivity: retain BF3-TS2-B1 as the completed-but-mode-pending topology comparison

drivers: one initial and one final analytic Hessian

limitations: A single starting topology can still converge to a wrong mode or a nearby saddle.
No IRC or endpoint identity is included.

optimization_controls.algorithm: single-guess Berny transition-state optimization with eigenvalue testing disabled during the search

method_profiles.relativistic_treatment: not required for B, C, F, H, N, and O

scf.stability_check: not included in the primary optimization

provenance: User-selected omega-B97XD/6-31G(d), SMD toluene base method for the BF3 benchmark sequence.
Numerical controls follow the reviewed BF3-TS1 standard protocol, replacing ReadFC with an initial analytic Hessian because B2 has no approved checkpoint.
Published BF3-TS2-B2 Cartesian geometry and C13-C21 target from DOI 10.1021/jacs.4c09067 SI.
options.purpose: Primary literature-aligned reproduction using an initial analytic Hessian, tight TS convergence, a restrained optimization step, and final analytic frequencies.

assumptions: Qualitative estimate only; the 78-atom analytic Hessians and optimization cycles dominate cost.
Protocol rigor and resource tier remain independent decisions.

acceptance_checks: normal termination and all Tight stationary-point criteria
fresh hash-bound manual review against C13-C21 bond formation
comparison with the reported -351.55 cm-1 feature without requiring numerical identity

validation_plan.claim_limit: Primary mode-consistent TS reproduction; no path or barrier validation.

exclusions: automatic continuation after a wrong or multiple-imaginary diagnostic
prerequisites: explicit approval of the neutral-singlet hypothesis
reviewed 78-atom literature geometry and C13-C21 atom map
accepted hash-bound BF3-TS2-B1 C13-C21 mode decision before rendering a B2 input
a normally terminated fixed-geometry diagnostic with a reviewed intended imaginary mode before reoptimization
applicability.reason: Provides stronger Hessian provenance and numerical convergence evidence at substantially higher cost.

coverage_plan.sensitivity: tighter grid, SCF, geometry convergence, and smaller step than standard
coverage_plan.structures: published BF3-TS2-B2 geometry with a hash-bound diagnostic-to-reoptimization chain

limitations: Strict is not an accuracy guarantee and can be slower without changing the chemical model.
The diagnostic does not itself establish a stationary point.
No IRC or endpoint identity is included.

provenance: User-selected omega-B97XD/6-31G(d), SMD toluene base method for the BF3 benchmark sequence.
Hessian-informed structure follows the successful BF3-TS1 recovery pattern but requires a fresh B2 diagnostic and approval.
Published BF3-TS2-B2 Cartesian geometry and C13-C21 target from DOI 10.1021/jacs.4c09067 SI.
options.purpose: Two-job Hessian-informed protocol: first diagnose the published geometry by analytic frequency, then bind that Hessian into a tighter, smaller-step TS reoptimization with final frequencies.

assumptions: Qualitative estimate only; two analytic frequency calculations plus a TS optimization are required.
Protocol rigor and resource tier remain independent decisions.

acceptance_checks: normal termination and complete 228-mode frequency analysis
manual review that the intended mode projects onto C13-C21
stop without reoptimization when the intended mode is absent or ambiguous
normal termination and all VeryTight stationary-point criteria
fresh hash-bound manual review against C13-C21 bond formation

validation_plan.claim_limit: Higher numerical rigor for one topology; still no path, barrier, or mechanism validation.
minimum_acceptance: accepted diagnostic mode before Hessian handoff

request_snapshot.claim_scope: One neutral closed-shell main-group first-order-saddle candidate whose sole imaginary mode must project onto C13-C21 bond formation; no IRC, barrier, topology ranking, or selectivity claim.
request_snapshot.goal: Prepare three reviewed offline protocols for reproducing the published BF3-TS2-B2 carbon-carbon bond-forming transition-state candidate.
workflow_prerequisites: The BF3-TS2-B1 result must receive an accepted hash-bound C13-C21 mode decision before a B2 Gaussian input is rendered.
The charge-zero singlet state remains an explicit B2 review proposal rather than source-reported Gaussian metadata.
B2 requires its own protocol selection, resource decision, fresh project, rendered-input hash review, and exact live approval.
